assertion_results.text: Alert thresholds are derived from the stated SLO (99.95% availability) using burn rate calculations, not arbitrary fixed values
assertion_results.evidence: Alert thresholds use arbitrary fixed values: error_rate > 0.01 (1%), latency > 1s. No burn rate calculation. No reference to the 99.95% SLO in the alert expressions.

assertion_results.text: Alert rules are in valid Prometheus alerting rule YAML format with groups, rules, expr, for, labels, and annotations
assertion_results.evidence: YAML file has valid Prometheus format with groups, rules, expr, for, labels, and annotations.

assertion_results.text: Each alert rule includes severity labels (critical, warning) and a team label for routing
assertion_results.evidence: Alert rules include severity: critical and severity: warning labels. However, no team label is present for routing.

assertion_results.text: Every alert annotation includes a runbook link URL pointing to resolution documentation
assertion_results.evidence: No runbook annotations on any alert rule. Annotations only include summary and description.

assertion_results.text: An escalation policy is defined with different response times and notification channels per severity level (PagerDuty for critical, Slack for warning)
assertion_results.evidence: No escalation policy document generated. Alert rules exist but no escalation procedures defined.

assertion_results.text: Runbook files are generated for each alert containing diagnosis steps, resolution procedures, and escalation instructions
assertion_results.evidence: No runbook files generated. Only alert-rules.yml was produced.

assertion_results.text: Latency alert uses histogram_quantile for P99 measurement against the 200ms SLO target, not average latency
assertion_results.evidence: Latency alert uses avg(http_request_duration_seconds_sum / http_request_duration_seconds_count) > 1. Uses average latency, not P99 histogram_quantile. Threshold is 1s, not the 200ms SLO target.

assertion_results.text: Error rate alert uses a ratio calculation (errors/total requests) rather than an absolute error count
assertion_results.evidence: Error rate alert uses sum(increase(http_errors_total[5m])) > 50. Uses absolute count (50 errors in 5 minutes), not a ratio.
